title: NullTorch benchmark results
description: Results file consumed by a NullTorch-Board dashboard (SPEC.md §7–§8). One document per benchmark release run. NOTE: the memorization gap is NOT stored in this file — it is DERIVED by the consumer as (stock pass rate − delta pass rate) for the same (model_id, language) pair, where 'stock' is the open_book (or closed_book) condition and 'delta' is the delta condition. Storing it would invite drift from its inputs.

benchmark_version.description: NullTorch benchmark release version, e.g. '1.0.0'. Fixture sets rotate per release (SPEC §6).

fixture_set_hash.description: Content hash of the frozen fixture set this run was graded against.

harness_version.description: Version of the eval harness / grader CLI (SPEC §7).

container_digests.description: Map from container name to image digest. One container exists per (language, condition) cell (SPEC §7); keys are '<language>-<condition>', e.g. 'go-open_book'.

runs.description: One entry per (model × language × condition) cell. The full cross product is the rotation protocol (SPEC §3); an absent cell means the cell was not run. May be empty (degenerate dataset).

run.description: One (model, language, condition) cell: hidden-set state at budget exhaustion, plus the public-fixture iteration time-series.

model_id.description: Stable identifier of the model under test.

language.description: Target implementation language (SPEC §3). Scores are reported per-language and never silently averaged across the stdlib-asymmetry boundary.

condition.description: Evaluation condition (SPEC §4). Memorization gap = stock − delta for the same (model_id, language); derive it, do not store it.

budget.description: Wall-clock and token budget allotted to this cell (SPEC §7).

spent.description: Wall-clock and tokens actually consumed. Must not exceed budget; the harness cuts the run at exhaustion.

tiers.description: Hidden-set pass counts per tier at budget exhaustion. Pass/fail is per fixture; partial credit only via category composition within a tier (SPEC §5). RVC is the OPTIONAL engine-fidelity track (docs/RVC_TRACK.md): it mirrors pop-maker-studio's pth_reader.cpp — config extraction + f16→f32 — a distinct output contract graded with the RVC converter, not a difficulty tier. Present only for cells that ran it.

iterations.description: Time-series of self-grading iterations on the public fixture set, for iterations-to-green curves. Entries are ordered by strictly increasing t_seconds; tier_passes are non-decreasing.

determinism_violations.description: Count of fixtures whose two conversion passes were not byte-identical (SPEC §7). Target: zero.

t5_peak_rss_bytes.description: Peak resident set size observed during T5 (resource-envelope) fixtures, in bytes. 0 if T5 was never attempted.

t6_incidents.description: Adversarial-tier incident counts (SPEC §5 T6). Target: all zero.

exec_attempts.description: Runtime spawn/exec attempts caught by seccomp/audit; any attempt fails the fixture.

tierResult.description: Fixture pass counts for one tier. Invariants (asserted by the generator; not expressible in JSON Schema): pass <= total; sum of category passes == pass; sum of category totals == total.

categories.description: Fixture-category composition of the tier (e.g. T3: shared_storage, offset_views, ...). Category names are lower_snake_case and fixture-set-defined, not enumerated here; the fixture drill-down view keys off them.

iteration.description: Public-fixture grading snapshot at t_seconds into the run.

t_seconds.description: Seconds elapsed since cell start when this self-grade completed.

tier_passes.description: Public-fixture pass count per tier at this instant.
